page: Amazon Web Services i4i.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core running at 3.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1875 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the i4i.2xlarge mostly in the average tier across standard compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1593.0 and a multi-core score of 5905.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 413.76 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 55.85 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak at 31.32 GB/sec. Additionally, while small-model LLM inference is average, processing medium-sized models is a notable bottleneck, yielding a poor prompt processing speed of 16.06 tokens/sec and a weak text generation speed of 7.44 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i4i.2xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe SSD storage and a balanced 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a viable option for workloads requiring fast local storage without the premium of dedicated high-memory instances. It is well-suited for database operations, as evidenced by its Passmark database score of 4013.63 and Redis SET performance of 8.49 million operations per second. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose storage-heavy applications, whereas it is not recommended for medium-to-large LLM inference or highly parallel decompression tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services i4i.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and 1875 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmarks indicate average performance across most compute and memory metrics, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5905.0 and Redis SET throughput of 8.49 million operations per second. However, tradeoffs include weak multi-threaded decompression and poor performance on medium-sized LLM inference tasks. Given its high local storage capacity and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-intensive workloads. It is best suited for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1875 GB local NVMe SSD storage, offering balanced database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.2xlarge is an x86_64 storage-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1875 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-capacity local NVMe SSDs, and a balanced memory-to-core ratio for database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 vCPUs and 3.5 GHz clock speed
1875 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Average performance in database operations and static web serving
Tradeoffs in multi-threaded decompression and medium LLM inference
Optimized for database hosting, web serving, and software compilation
